rationale.R1.2: There is a structural tension between the rigorous methodology described (Wilcoxon + Holm, 6,980 queries, majority wins across all four pairings) and the near-total refusal to commit to conclusions in paragraphs 3–6. The premises support stronger inferences than the stated conclusions, which constitutes a logical consistency problem regardless of the intended task.
rationale.R1.3: The statistical narrative sets up load-bearing claims (non-trivial signed-rank statistics, majority-query dominance) that the prose then systematically walks back with compounding hedges. Assessing whether the conclusions actually track the reported evidence is central to any meaningful review of this passage.
rationale.R1.6: The final four paragraphs exhibit pathological over-hedging ('seems to suggest that the proposed model may perhaps offer some degree', 'tentatively conclude that the reranker tends to behave at least somewhat competitively'). Whether the goal is rewriting or evaluation, the prose quality is independently impaired and directly visible in the artifact.
